branch_metadata.branch_rationale: Structural / loop-class closure repository default fallback

description: Documented reclassification cases. Reclassification of an observable from sigma_alt to sigma_new is allowed under rules R1-R5 (Section 3.5 of the paper); more than two reclassifications per observable, or violation of any of R1-R5, falsifies the theorem under triggers F1-F3.

rules.R1: Falsification trigger: sigma_alt was falsified by pipeline data (discrepancy > 100 x the program's PG tolerance = 100 x 2.5%).
rules.R2: Algorithm consistency: sigma_new follows from the deterministic mapping algorithm under an explicit re-identification of (n, g, s). The re-identification must be physically motivated.
rules.R3: Finiteness: at most TWO reclassifications per observable. More than two falsifies the loop-class completeness theorem (theorem-fall).
rules.R4: Cross-sector pre-specification: the new class sigma_new must already appear independently as a closure in at least ONE other sector cluster. No ad-hoc reclassification.
rules.R5: Own falsification bound: sigma_new must define its own falsification bound that is checked in the pipeline.

theorem_falsification_triggers.F1: An observable in G_claim^auth cannot be assigned to any L_sigma in the library, even after the R3-bounded reclassification budget, that is consistent with the pipeline target. (R3 violations are captured here.)
theorem_falsification_triggers.F2: A negative-control observable is assigned a closing class in the library whose post-loop value lies within the PRECISE_2.5 cut of its experimental target. The algorithm has then over-claimed and the scope-discipline guarantee fails.
theorem_falsification_triggers.F3: Cross-sector consistency breaks: a class L_sigma that is verified in one sector falsifies in another, and the two sectors cannot be discriminated by their (n_sigma, g_sigma, s_sigma) topology factors.
theorem_falsification_triggers._input_validation_note: The case 'mapping returns more than one valid loop class for the same (n,g,s)' is an INPUT-VALIDATION error (the topology triple was malformed, the binary discriminator flags w (double-Wick) and r (resummed) were not read) — it is NOT a theorem-falsification trigger and is handled by classify_observable.py's ValueError path.

documented_cases.R1_reason: Pipeline discrepancy >> 100 x sigma_PG = 250%; clearly falsified.
documented_cases.R2_reason: Re-identification of (n, g, s): SN distance dominates by propagator resummation on cosmological scales -> n=4 (full spinor-trace), corresponding to Lemma 4.
documented_cases.R4_reason: Lemma-4 class already used independently for T_RH; not ad-hoc.
documented_cases.R5_bound: Lemma-4 prediction is sin^2(pi*gamma^3) ~ 1e-5; would falsify if pipeline reports >> 1e-4.
